methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Nominal is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Cameron McCord and Bryce Strauss, currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $155M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), all operating in the same sector and stage.

Two valuation methods triangulate on Nominal's fair value. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $1.9B. Peer-median analysis anchors to four same-stage AI & Software peers—Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), and Photoroom ($500M)—producing a median valuation of $5.3B. The wide peer range ($500M to $32.0B) reflects material dispersion in stage-B AI company valuations.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.6B to $6.1B, with a median of $3.6B. The current $1.0B valuation sits at the lower bound of this range, roughly 28% below the triangulated median. Key uncertainties include the applicability of both the stage-progression multiplier and peer median given the extreme heterogeneity in Series B AI valuations—evidenced by the 64x spread between Photoroom and Safe Superintelligence. Capital efficiency, product-market validation metrics, and the specific competitive positioning of Nominal relative to this diverse peer set would materially influence placement within the derived range.
